title: transparency log + equivocation mitigation
manager: An append-only public log that makes certificate claims tamper-evident — plus mitigation for the equivocation attack, where a log shows different views to different people. Offline inclusion proofs alone can't detect such a fork, so the mitigation is witness cosigning or gossip. This is what makes "logged when" actually trustworthy: a claim in the log can't be quietly retracted or shown selectively to one party and hidden from another.
cs: Rekor-or-self-hosted log (decision from the security brainstorm), inclusion proofs self-contained for offline; equivocation mitigation via witness cosigning or gossip; the tamper-evidence layer.
